2016-11-01.title: Choi Soon-sil, at Center of Political Scandal in South Korea, Is Jailed
2016-11-01.abstract: Ms. Choi, a friend of President Park Geun-hye’s for 40 years, was grilled by prosecutors over allegations that she had manipulated important government affairs from the shadows.

2016-11-03.abstract: The recording by Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi, the professed caliph of the Islamic State, was his first in nearly a year and came days after Iraqi troops entered Mosul.

2016-11-25.title: Sandboarding in Peru
2016-11-25.abstract: Peruvian teenagers dream of snowboarding. There’s only one problem: There’s no snow. Watch how they make do with sand instead.

2016-11-25.title: Which Language Uses the Most Sounds? Click 5 Times for the Answer
2016-11-25.abstract: Taa, spoken by a few thousand people in Botswana and Namibia, is believed to have the largest sound inventory of any language in the world.

2016-11-25.title: In Scotland, Trump Built a Wall. Then He Sent Residents the Bill.
2016-11-25.abstract: The president-elect left a trail of unmet promises on one of his golf courses, and lashed out at homeowners who stood in his way.

2016-11-25.title: Hungry Venezuelans Flee in Boats to Escape Economic Collapse
2016-11-25.abstract: Well over 150,000 people have fled Venezuela in the last year alone, the most in more than a decade, scholars say, with the sea route posing special dangers.

2016-11-26.title: U.S. Officials Defend Integrity of Vote, Despite Hacking Fears
2016-11-26.abstract: Some opponents of Donald J. Trump are seeking recounts in Wisconsin, Michigan and Pennsylvania, states where his margin of victory was extremely thin.

2016-11-26.title: Ukraine’s Ex-Leader Regrets Not Breaking Up Protests That Led to His Fall
2016-11-26.abstract: Viktor F. Yanukovych, the former president of Ukraine, said that his “main mistake” was not declaring martial law, but that he did not want to cause a civil war.

2016-11-26.title: Scott Dayton Identified as First American to Die in Syria Combat
2016-11-26.abstract: Chief Dayton, who lived in Virginia and was assigned to a bomb-disposal unit, was killed in northern Syria on Thursday by an improvised bomb.

2016-11-26.title: My Three Days With Fidel
2016-11-26.abstract: Richard Eder, a former New York Times foreign correspondent, recalls interviewing the Cuban leader.

2016-11-26.title: Fidel Castro, Cuban Revolutionary Who Defied U.S., Dies at 90
2016-11-26.abstract: Mr. Castro brought the Cold War to the Western Hemisphere, bedeviled 11 American presidents and briefly pushed the world to the brink of nuclear war.

2016-11-27.title: Half a Million Children Are Trapped in Syria, United Nations Says
2016-11-27.abstract: A report by Unicef said the children were among hundreds of thousands of people in besieged areas who were cut off from humanitarian aid and basic services.

2016-11-27.title: One Family. Six Decades. Myriad Views of Fidel Castro’s Revolution.
2016-11-27.abstract: The clash of emotions common in Cuban families is reflected in a grandfather’s loyalty to Mr. Castro and a granddaughter’s glum view of conditions in her country.

2016-11-27.title: Subdued Cuba Prepares Memorial for Castro
2016-11-27.abstract: A two-day memorial and a period of “Duelo Nacional,” or national mourning, will offer Cubans a chance to pay their respects to the longtime leader.

2016-11-27.title: Israel Defense Forces Kill 4 ISIS-Linked Attackers in Golan Heights
2016-11-27.abstract: The firefight, near Syria, appeared to be the first deadly exchange between Israeli forces and fighters associated with the Islamic State.

2016-11-27.title: Afghan Vice President Seen Abducting Rival
2016-11-27.abstract: The vice president, Gen. Abdul Rashid Dostum, is accused of having a political rival beaten “in a barbaric way” at a sporting event, before kidnapping him.

2016-11-28.title: François Fillon Wins Center-Right Nomination for French Presidency
2016-11-28.abstract: Mr. Fillon, a former prime minister who pledged to limit immigration and control Islam, handily defeated Alain Juppé in a Republican Party runoff.

2016-11-28.title: South Korea’s Impeachment Process, Explained
2016-11-28.abstract: Lawmakers say they will try to force President Park Geun-hye out of office. Here is how the process works.

2016-11-28.title: Animosity in a Burmese Hub Deepens as Chinese Get Richer
2016-11-28.abstract: The Chinese dominate the economy in Mandalay, Myanmar, a trading town known for dealing in minerals, timber and drugs.

2016-11-28.title: Can Oil Help Mexico Withstand Trump’s Attack on Trade? It’s Hard to See How
2016-11-28.abstract: The country’s state-owned oil company, Pemex, is saddled with debt and struggling with production. As a Donald J. Trump presidency looms, fixing it is a top priority.

2016-11-28.title: Fidel Castro’s Sister, an Outspoken Critic, Takes No Joy in His Death
2016-11-28.abstract: Juanita Castro, who fled Cuba decades ago and became a vocal critic of her brother’s regime, expressed disdain for those who rejoiced over his death.

2016-11-29.title: Women in Canadian Military Report Widespread Sexual Assault
2016-11-29.abstract: A survey commissioned by the armed forces puts hard numbers on a 2015 report that showed a culture hostile toward women and other minorities.

2016-11-29.title: Amid Flames and Suspicions in Israel, Acts of Jewish-Arab Cooperation
2016-11-29.abstract: The wildfires that swept across Israel’s countryside last week brought claims of arson, but also an outpouring of good will.

2016-11-29.title: Houthis, in Surprise Move, Form a Government in Yemen
2016-11-29.abstract: The announcement angered the movement’s Saudi-backed rivals and threatened diplomatic efforts to end a conflict that has ravaged Yemen for nearly two years.

2016-11-29.title: For Bashar al-Assad, Winning the Syrian War May Lead to New Troubles
2016-11-29.abstract: If the Syrian president defeats the rebels, he may preside over an economic wasteland hampered by a low-level insurgency and facing a challenge to find financial aid to rebuild.

2016-11-29.title: Mark Taimanov, World-Class Chess and Piano Player, Dies at 90
2016-11-29.abstract: Mr. Taimanov was a classical pianist and former Soviet chess champion whose lopsided loss to Bobby Fischer in 1971 cost him his government salary.

2016-11-30.title: ‘Unintentional’ Human Error Led to Airstrikes on Syrian Troops, Pentagon Says
2016-11-30.abstract: According to an official inquiry, the American-led attacks in September were conducted under the “good-faith belief” that the targets were Islamic State militants.

2016-11-30.title: Justin Trudeau Approves Oil Pipeline Expansion in Canada
2016-11-30.abstract: The Kinder Morgan Trans Mountain project would link the oil sands in Alberta to a tanker port in British Columbia, where there have been several protests against the plan.

2016-11-30.title: French Election Hints at a European Shift Toward Russia
2016-11-30.abstract: François Fillon’s victory in a presidential primary suggests that a shift from confrontation to accommodation of Moscow has already begun in the region.

2016-11-30.title: Michael Flynn, a Top Trump Adviser, Ties China and North Korea to Jihadists
2016-11-30.abstract: By appointing General Flynn, President-elect Donald J. Trump has signaled that he intends to prioritize policy on the Middle East and Islamist militants.

2016-11-30.title: On Castro’s Old Campus, Students Pay Tribute
2016-11-30.abstract: Hundreds of students joined a memorial service at the University of Havana, where Fidel Castro had immersed himself in radical politics in the 1940s.
